item.title: Manyame warns against illegal land deals @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Manyame warns against illegal land deals -Newsday Zimbabwe THE Manyame Rural District Council (RDC) has issued a strong warning against the illegal occupation and sale of land in the Seke Communal Area and surrounding peri-urban farms, saying bogus co-operatives and land barons are defrauding unsuspecting home-seekers using forged documents. The warning comes amid a [...] The post Manyame warns against illegal land deals appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Wicknell Chivayo company, Chinese partner win $2.9 billion Kenya airport tender @zimsituation @wicknellchivayo
item.description: IMC Construction is a joint venture partner with China’s state-owned conglomerate Source: Wicknell Chivayo company, Chinese partner win $2.9 billion Kenya airport tender – Zimbabwe News Now Wicknell Chivayo HARARE — Wicknell Chivayo’s company IMC Construction Kenya has won a stake in a $2.9 billion tender to expand Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, ZimLive has established, [...] The post Wicknell Chivayo company, Chinese partner win $2.9 billion Kenya airport tender appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Premier African Minerals mobilises US$1.07 million for Zimbabwe projects
item.description: Premier African Minerals has raised approximately $1.07 million before expenses through a share subscription to fund ongoing optimisation and operational activities at its Zulu Lithium and Tantalum Project in Zimbabwe. The AIM-listed company announced on 11 June that the funding was secured through the issue of new ordinary shares at 0.02 pence each. The proceeds [...] The post Premier African Minerals mobilises US$1.07 million for Zimbabwe projects appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Parliament resets interview date to fill ZEC Commissioner vacancy
item.description: THE Parliament of Zimbabwe has postponed interviews for candidates seeking appointment to the Zimbabwe Electoral Commission (ZEC), with the selection process now set for June 24, 2026. In a public notice issued Monday, Parliament said the interviews for the four shortlisted applicants, Muchineripi Chinyanganya, Sheuneni Kurasha, Dr Lawrence Mhandara and Advent Tatenda, had been rescheduled [...] The post Parliament resets interview date to fill ZEC Commissioner vacancy appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: SA: Zimbabwean man loses court bid to overturn Home Affairs ban over fake permanent resident permit
item.description: The Citizen The Western Cape High Court has dismissed a Zimbabwean man’s application for an extension of the 180-day period to review the decision by the department of Home Affairs to declare him a prohibited person. The 40-year-old Fidel Isheanesu Mugunzva was born in Zimbabwe. He first entered South Africa on 11 September 2011 on [...] The post SA: Zimbabwean man loses court bid to overturn Home Affairs ban over fake permanent resident permit appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zim eyes US$1bn in manufactured exports by 2030
item.description: Oliver Kazunga-Senior Reporter ZIMBABWE has set an ambitious target to almost double the value of manufactured exports to US$1 billion by 2030 as the Government intensifies efforts to accelerate industrialisation,... (via Ebusiness Weekly) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Ebusiness Weekly

item.title: Sonja Left Shocked After High Court Rules She Was Never Married To Wicknell Chivayo Despite Years Together @wicknellchivayo
item.description: High Court Delivers Surprise Ruling In Chivayo And Sonja Dispute Sonja Madzikanda says she was left shocked after the High Court reportedly ruled that she was never married to businessman Wicknell Chivayo. The decision, announced on Monday, 15 June 2026, marks the latest chapter in a lengthy legal battle between the former couple over the [...] The post Sonja Left Shocked After High Court Rules She Was Never Married To Wicknell Chivayo Despite Years Together appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Harare Resident Sues City Of Harare To Stop Prepaid Water Meters
item.description: City Of Harare Sued Over Prepaid Water Meters A Harare resident has taken the City of Harare and a private water company to the High Court in a bid to stop the rollout of prepaid water meters, arguing that the system is not supported by the city’s existing water laws. The legal challenge was announced [...] The post Harare Resident Sues City Of Harare To Stop Prepaid Water Meters appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwe reviews Liquor Licence Act @bulawayo24
item.description: The Government has launched a nationwide review of Zimbabwe’s liquor licensing framework, with stakeholder consultations underway as authorities seek to modernise legislation, improve regulatory effic... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Israel Ebola travel ban on five African states sparks diplomatic row with Kenya
item.description: JERUSALEM/NAIROBI – Israel has introduced sweeping travel restrictions on foreign nationals from five African countries over Ebola-related concerns, triggering diplomatic backlash from Kenya, which insists it has recorded no cases of the disease and calls the move unjustified. Under a directive issued by Israel’s Population and Immigration Authority, airlines operating flights to Israel have been [...] The post Israel Ebola travel ban on five African states sparks diplomatic row with Kenya appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Oil prices plunge as US and Iran announce peace deal – is cheaper fuel coming at last?
item.description: Cheaper fuel could be on the horizon for the world and South Africa after a peace deal was reached between the US and Iran, which is expected to see the Strait of Hormuz reopening toll-free. The news sent global stocks soaring on Monday, while oil prices tumbled on the news. The two sides confirmed an [...] The post Oil prices plunge as US and Iran announce peace deal – is cheaper fuel coming at last? appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zimbabwe turns to gold, platinum and lithium exports as economic buffer against global geopolitical shocks
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe is banking on a surge in mineral export earnings and a strategic shift toward value-added processing to cushion its economy from mounting global uncertainty triggered by escalating tensions in the Middle East. Government projections indicate that mineral exports could generate between US$6.5 billion and US$7 billion this year, providing a vital source [...] The post Zimbabwe turns to gold, platinum and lithium exports as economic buffer against global geopolitical shocks appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Iran and US reach an initial deal to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz but challenges remain
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates — The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement Monday that would extend their shaky ceasefire and lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but significant challenges remain to ending the war, including whether Israel will continue its offensive in Lebanon. Details of the deal were not immediately [...] The post Iran and US reach an initial deal to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz but challenges remain appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: US stocks jump to their best day in 2 months on hopes for a deal to get crude flowing globally again
item.description: NEW YORK — U.S. stocks rallied to their best day in two months, and oil prices fell Thursday after President Donald Trump called off his threat to bomb Iran in the evening. That raised hopes for a potential deal that could get the global flow of oil going again. The S&P 500 jumped 1.8%, coming [...] The post US stocks jump to their best day in 2 months on hopes for a deal to get crude flowing globally again appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Third Mozambican suspect in Kruger Park double murder arrested in Zimbabwe
item.description: A third suspect linked to the brutal murder of a couple in the Kruger National Park has been arrested in Zimbabwe, marking a significant breakthrough in a complex cross-border investigation. The South African Police Service (SAPS) confirmed that the 26-year-old Mozambican national, identified as Macandze Lionel da Marta, was apprehended after evading authorities and fleeing [...] The post Third Mozambican suspect in Kruger Park double murder arrested in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo

item.title: Can the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX) Revitalize Zimbabwe’s Economy?
item.description: Can the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX) Revitalize Zimbabwe’s Economy? — via Foreign Policy Research Institute #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Foreign Policy Research Institute

item.title: Pardon Mapondera: gone but not forgotten at Venice
item.description: The fifth and final piece in our series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Pardon Mapondera, the artist whose practice the room finally arranges itself around, treats discarded plastic and glass jars not as ecological signal but as ritual matter, and produces, in the process, the most quietly devotional installation in the Biennale this year. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Gideon Gomo: from stone to bronze, the ancestral algorithm at Venice
item.description: The fourth in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Gideon Gomo’s first sustained body of bronze work, paired with discarded vehicle number plates and Zimbabwean springstone, is the move of a sculptor at full maturity rethinking the material vocabulary of his country’s most internationally legible art form. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
item.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist’s day-long procession through the city’s campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice. #Zimbabwe #Culture #ZimbabweCulture #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Franklyn Dzingai: family albums and the neural commons at Venice
item.description: The third in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Franklyn Dzingai’s reductive cardboard prints, built from the family album, the magazine clipping, the back-room studio photographs of mid-century Zimbabwe, turn out, on close looking, to be the most precisely engineered work in the room. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Felix Shumba: charcoal and the practice of disappearance at Venice
item.description: The second in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Felix Shumba’s six-metre charcoal wall and his Telephone Reader series anchor a body of work that argues drawing is not the act of capturing the seen, but the act of staying present to what is on its way out of sight. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Eva Raath: a slow archive of stitched memory at Venice
item.description: In the first of five long-form pieces on the Pavilion of Zimbabwe at #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Isn’t it lovely: a week in Venice with the Pavilion of Zimbabwe
item.description: A letter from Venice, written in the wake of the opening of Zimbabwe’s #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Valentine Eluwasi
